title: viola pedunculata subsp. typica
intro: Viola pedunculata subsp. typica, also known as the common or typical yellow violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet genus that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Viola pedunculata subsp. typica include its potential to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its antioxidant and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, or dried to make a powder that can be added to salves and creams for topical use.
